methodology.cell_position: ME(R) ROI centroid per body, in raw 8-nm voxel units, averaged from neuron_polylines.parquet.

home_column_methods.canonical: For each cell, every ME(R) post-synapse (from syn-partners-2024-09-11-a7d912.feather) is classified to its nearest column anchor (892 anchors from column_anchors_3d.parquet, KD-tree NN). The cell's canonical home column is the column receiving the most ME(R) synapses (majority vote). Reported alongside is the fraction of ME(R) synapses in the home column ("home_purity") — closer to 1 means the cell's dendrite is highly localised to one column; closer to 1/N means the dendrite spans many columns.
home_column_methods.L4_dominance: For L4 specifically, the home cartridge is computed as in Carol's per_cartridge_L4_triangle_summary methodology: each L4's home cartridge is the column where its dominant L1 post-synaptic partner lives (L1 is itself canonically assigned by the synapse-majority method above). For L4 cells with no L1 post-target (347 of 891) the canonical method is used instead.

methodology.edge_set: All directed synapse edges (a→b) within the same cell type from edges_w1.parquet — no symmetrisation, no threshold above 1.

simplex_definitions.1_simplex: an ordered directed edge a→b with weight ≥ w
simplex_definitions.2_simplex: a transitive triple (a, b, c) with a→b, b→c, and a→c all of weight ≥ w (Reimann 2017 directed flag complex)
simplex_definitions.3_simplex: a 4-tuple (a, b, c, d) admitting a linear order such that a→b, a→c, a→d, b→c, b→d, c→d are all of weight ≥ w (directed acyclic tournament)
